description: AGT-02: when the user states a business problem, the assistant leads with the business outcome and proposes a concrete play — it does NOT open with an inventory of connected accounts/setup, nor with a 'which of your existing automations did you mean?' menu. Pins the business-intent-first doctrine. Judgment-heavy -> capability.
userTurns: leads keep slipping through the cracks after the demo — half of them never get a follow-up

judge.rubric: The user described a business pain (leads not getting followed up after the demo). PASS when the assistant engages with that OUTCOME first — naming the problem and proposing a concrete way to fix it (e.g. an automated follow-up sequence for post-demo leads) and moving toward building/doing it. Asking ONE business framing question (which CRM/inbox holds these leads, what the follow-up should say) is fine. FAIL when the opener is instead a dump of the user's connected accounts/setup as the main content, OR a 'which of your existing automations did you mean?' style menu, OR a generic 'what would you like me to do?' that ignores the stated outcome. The test: does it talk about the user's problem, or about the app's inventory/menus?
